name: Vercel
description: Frontend cloud platform created by the makers of Next.js, offering full-stack deployment with edge-first architecture and serverless capabilities

description.value: Frontend cloud platform created by the makers of Next.js, offering full-stack deployment with edge-first architecture and serverless capabilities

parent-company.comment: Independent company founded by Guillermo Rauch. Originally named ZEIT (2015), rebranded to Vercel in April 2020

year-launched.comment: Founded in 2015 as ZEIT, rebranded to Vercel in April 2020

open-source.comment: The Vercel platform itself is proprietary. However, Vercel maintains many open-source projects including Next.js, Turborepo, and the Vercel CLI

primary-focus.comment: Originally a JAMstack platform, now positions itself as a full-stack frontend cloud with strong edge-first capabilities

free-tier.comment: Hobby plan is free forever but limited to personal, non-commercial use

free-tier-bandwidth.comment: Hobby plan includes 100 GB bandwidth per month

free-tier-sites.comment: No limit on number of projects on the Hobby plan

paid-plan-starting-price.comment: Pro plan at $20/user/month. Includes free Viewer seats for non-deploying members

bandwidth-overage-cost.comment: Pro plan includes 1 TB/month, then $0.15/GB. Hobby overage is $20 per 100 GB

enterprise-pricing.comment: Enterprise plan with custom pricing, 99.99% SLA, and dedicated support

git-integration.comment: Native integrations for GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket. Azure DevOps via official extension. Self-hosted Git can be used via CLI with vercel build/deploy --prebuilt

auto-deploy.comment: Automatic deployments on every branch push and merge to production branch

cli-deployment.comment: Vercel CLI supports deploy, build, rollback, and other operations

build-system.comment: Built-in CI/CD build pipeline with framework auto-detection for 50+ frameworks

supported-frameworks.comment: Auto-detects 50+ frameworks including Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby, Astro, Hugo, Jekyll, SvelteKit, Remix, Angular, Vue, Eleventy, Vite, and many more. Deepest integration with Next.js

monorepo-support.comment: Supports Turborepo, Nx, Yarn Workspaces, and pnpm Workspaces. Can configure root directory and ignored build steps per project

deploy-previews.comment: Every push to a non-production branch creates a unique preview deployment URL

instant-rollbacks.comment: Instant Rollback is generally available on all plans via dashboard, CLI, or API

deploy-notifications.comment: Slack integration for deployment status, email alerts, webhook alerts, and GitHub deployment status checks

cdn-provider.comment: Built on AWS CloudFront infrastructure with proprietary Edge Network layer. Static files stored on S3, DNS managed via Route 53

edge-locations.comment: 119 Points of Presence in 94 cities across 51 countries, with 18 compute-capable edge regions

http2.comment: HTTP/2 is automatically enabled for all deployments

http3.comment: HTTP/3 (QUIC) is not natively supported by Vercel as of early 2026. Can be achieved by placing Cloudflare in front

response-headers.comment: Custom response headers can be configured via vercel.json or framework config

image-optimization.comment: Built-in Image Optimization with automatic format conversion, resizing, and quality adjustment. Best integration with Next.js Image component

asset-compression.comment: Both gzip and Brotli compression supported. Brotli takes precedence when client supports it

free-ssl.comment: SSL certificates automatically provisioned via Let's Encrypt at no cost for all deployments

wildcard-ssl.comment: Wildcard SSL supported when using Vercel's nameservers for DNS management

auto-https-redirect.comment: All HTTP requests automatically redirected to HTTPS using 308 status code. Cannot be disabled

custom-domain-preview.comment: Preview deployments can have custom subdomain aliases via wildcard domain configuration

serverless-functions.comment: First-party serverless functions that scale from zero to thousands of concurrent executions

functions-runtime.comment: Supports Node.js, Python, Go, Ruby for serverless functions. Edge Functions use V8 runtime (JavaScript/TypeScript/WebAssembly)

edge-functions.comment: Edge Functions run globally on the V8 engine with low latency (10-50ms). Unified under Vercel Functions in 2025

scheduled-functions.comment: Vercel Cron Jobs allow scheduling serverless functions. Only run on production deployments

form-handling.comment: No built-in form handling like Netlify Forms. Requires third-party services like Formspree or custom serverless function endpoints

authentication.comment: No built-in user authentication/identity management. Relies on third-party providers like Auth0, Clerk via marketplace integrations. Vercel Authentication exists only for deployment protection

kv-storage.comment: Edge Config provides ultra-low latency key-value reads for feature flags and configuration. Original Vercel KV (Redis) has been sunset but available via Upstash integration with unified billing

database-integration.comment: Original Vercel Postgres (Neon) has been sunset. Database integrations now available through Vercel Marketplace with providers like Neon, Supabase, PlanetScale, and others with unified billing

redirects.comment: Supports 301, 302, 307, 308 redirects via vercel.json or framework-level configuration

rewrites.comment: URL rewrites and reverse proxying supported via vercel.json configuration

custom-404.comment: Custom 404 pages supported via framework conventions or static 404.html file

spa-fallback.comment: SPA fallback routing configured via rewrites in vercel.json to serve index.html for all paths

headers-config.comment: Custom response headers configurable per-path or globally via vercel.json headers configuration

ab-testing.comment: A/B testing via Edge Middleware and Edge Config for feature flags. Integrations with Split, LaunchDarkly, Statsig, and ConfigCat

free-team-members.comment: Hobby plan is solo-only for personal use. Team features require Pro plan ($20/user/month)

rbac.comment: Role-Based Access Control with multiple roles per user. Extended RBAC with fine-grained permissions on Enterprise plans

audit-logs.comment: Audit Logs available on Enterprise plans to track events performed by team members

environment-variables.comment: Environment variables configurable per environment (production, preview, development) via dashboard, CLI, or API

preview-comments.comment: Visual commenting system on preview deployments, similar to Figma. Supports markdown, @mentions, screenshots, and Slack sync

ddos-protection.comment: Automatic DDoS mitigation on all plans via Protectd infrastructure. Analyzes ~550K events/second with median mitigation time of 2.5 seconds

waf.comment: Web Application Firewall available free on all plans. Supports custom rules to log, block, challenge, or rate limit traffic. Protects against SQL injection, XSS, and RFI

soc2.comment: SOC 2 Type II attestation. Also ISO 27001:2022 certified

gdpr.comment: GDPR compliant with appropriate technical and organizational security measures, data breach notification, and data subject rights support

password-protection.comment: Password Protection available on Pro and Enterprise plans for preview and production deployments

ip-access-control.comment: Trusted IPs feature restricts deployment access to allowlisted IP addresses. Available on Enterprise plan

documentation-quality.comment: Exceptional documentation: comprehensive, well-organized, searchable, with tutorials, API reference, guides, knowledge base, and Vercel Academy. Regularly updated

integrations.comment: Extensive marketplace with categories including Analytics, CMS, Monitoring/Observability, Authentication, Database, Commerce, and Search. Also includes AI, Storage, DevTools, and more

community.comment: Community forum at community.vercel.com, GitHub Discussions for vercel/vercel and vercel/next.js, active Stack Overflow presence. Discord available for specific projects like v0

status-page.comment: Public status page at vercel-status.com with real-time and historical system performance data
